Downscaling for Climate Scenarios

Developed by Tragsatec for AEMET

Home Data Methodology Evaluation Downscaling Applications Information

ABOUT

The main objective of this project is to perform statistical downscaling of as many climate variables as possible for available climate change scenarios for the Iberian Peninsula.

Statistical downscaling is a technique that allows obtaining very high-resolution climate information from global climate models by establishing statistical relationships between large-scale variables and local observations.

First, essential climate variables that could provide the maximum possible information with the minimum number of data points were selected. Therefore, temperature, precipitation, wind speed, relative humidity, and short-wave surface radiation were chosen.

Since some of these variables have few local observations, an evaluation was carried out in the Iberian Peninsula with several high-resolution reanalyses to determine which dataset most closely resembles AEMET observations and is therefore the most suitable for performing downscaling with a large number of data points.

Statistical downscaling of our five variables was performed on an extended ensemble of eleven global models.

PROJECT TEAM & FUNDING

This project was funded through a service contract in which Tragsatec (Tragsa Group) acted as a subcontractor for the Climate Evaluation and Modeling Area at the Spanish State Meteorological Agency (AEMET).

Work was carried out by Irene Rodríguez Muñoz, Jesús Gutiérrez Fernández, and María Ortega Camacho, and supervised by Esteban Rodríguez Guisado.

pyClim-SDM software was developed by Alfonso Hernanz.

pyGridEval calculation tool was developed by Carlos Correa.
